summary.intent: Add GitHub artifact attestations to the moltis release workflow (following Secretive's pattern) and document the attestation verification process in README, SECURITY.md, and the docs site.
summary.outcome: Attestations were added to three workflow jobs (upload-release, merge-docker, generate-sbom) using SHA-pinned actions/attest-build-provenance@v4, docs were updated across four files, and the changes were committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: The release workflow has three distinct jobs that produce artifacts: upload-release (binaries), merge-docker (multi-arch OCI image), and generate-sbom (CycloneDX + SPDX).
The build-docker job already had attestations: write permission; upload-release and generate-sbom needed it added.
A sign-artifacts composite action exists at .github/actions/sign-artifacts/action.yml handling GPG and cosign signing.
Security documentation is split across three locations: README.md, SECURITY.md (root), docs/src/security.md, and docs/src/release-verification.md.
The project is under the moltis-org GitHub organization, so attestations will be at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/attestations.

code.finding: Container attestation in merge-docker requires push-to-registry: true so the attestation is attached to the OCI image in GHCR rather than uploaded as a separate artifact.

workflow: actions/attest-build-provenance v4 and v4.1.0 both resolve to SHA a2bbfa25375fe432b6a289bc6b6cd05ecd0c4c32; always pin to SHA for supply chain security.
A security hook blocked the permissions edit on first attempt; retrying the edit succeeded — hooks may require the edit content to pass an inspection before allowing it.
Secretive's release.yml is a useful reference implementation for attest-build-provenance usage patterns in a real project.

friction: A security hook blocked the permissions edit to release.yml on the first attempt, requiring a retry.
Multiple partial reads of release.yml were needed to understand the full job structure — the file was read in many separate chunks across the session.
Resolving the correct SHA for actions/attest-build-provenance@v4 required multiple Bash commands (tag lookup, annotated-tag dereference) before getting the right commit hash.
open_items: The PR was created but not yet reviewed or merged — attestations won't be active in releases until it lands.
No end-to-end test of the attestation flow was possible without actually running the release workflow; correctness of the YAML additions is unverified beyond syntax validation.
The docs/src/SUMMARY.md was read but it's unclear if a new nav entry for attestation verification was added there — may need a follow-up check.
